question.text: What is the maximum contribution a single Non-Resident Indian (NRI) or Overseas Citizen of India (OCI) can make in the corpus of an FPI applicant?

answer.text: Below 50 percent of the total contribution in the corpus
Below 10 percent of the total contribution in the corpus
Below 15 percent of the total contribution in the corpus
Below 25 percent of the total contribution in the corpus

explanation.text: **The Logic:** As per SEBI (Foreign Portfolio Investors) Regulations, 2019, the contribution of a single NRI, OCI, or Resident Indian individual should be below 25 percent of the total contribution in the corpus of the applicant. **The Trap:** Candidates may confuse the single NRI/OCI limit (below 25%) with the aggregate limit (below 50%) for all NRIs, OCIs, and RI individuals combined. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Remember the 25-50 rule: single NRI/OCI below 25%, aggregate NRI+OCI+RI below 50%.
